note: One creative reused across Instagram, Facebook & Google Business Profile. 10 posts to choose from, diversified by format/hook/topic, each modelled on a niche over-performer and rewritten in EHN clinician voice.

posts.visual_recipe: Solid purple background (#6B5B95) with centered blue butterfly illustration (bright cyan with black outline) positioned in upper-center. Below the butterfly, bold white sans-serif heading "World Thyroid Day" (large, ~48-60px) followed by validating subheading "You are not alone in this." (~32-36px, medium weight). Small right-arrow icon in light circle positioned bottom-right corner. Clean, minimalist layout with ample whitespace, ~5 visible words on image.
posts.angle: Thyroid health validation: you're not alone in managing thyroid dysfunction.
posts.build_brief:
Create a square carousel card (single card or repeatable slide format). 

STRUCTURE & COMPOSITION (replicating the winner's layout exactly):
- Background: soft sage-cream blend (approximately #F5F9F7, a warm neutral that harmonises with EHN green without competing). Ensures ample whitespace and clinical calm.
- PRIMARY VISUAL ANCHOR: EHN green #39B54A positioned as a vertical accent stripe or soft shape in the left-third or upper-left corner—replacing the competitor's butterfly with a grounded functional-medicine visual: a simple, minimalist thyroid gland outline illustration in solid green #39B54A (line weight ~2–3px, no fill, editorial style—not clip-art). Position this illustration in the upper-centre area, slightly left of dead-centre, mirroring the winner's vertical balance.
- HEADLINE PLACEMENT: Centred, below the illustration, in Satoshi Bold 700, approximately 52–60px. Main text in charcoal (#2C2C2C). ONE meaningful word ("Thyroid" or "Supported") in green #39B54A italic to anchor the brand colour.
- SUBHEADING: Directly below, in Satoshi Medium 500, approximately 32–36px, charcoal. Validating tone: "You are not alone in this" (or similar, replicating the winner's emotional register).
- SECONDARY ACCENT: Small icon (tick mark, leaf, or compass point) in soft teal (#5DADE2 or a warm sage-grey) positioned bottom-right corner in a subtle 24–28px circle, matching the winner's "arrow" device but in EHN's secondary palette.
- WHITESPACE: Replicate the winner's minimalist density—approximately 5–7 visible words on the image, generous margins (20–30% of canvas), clean breathing room between elements.
- DENSITY & HIERARCHY: Very high contrast between headline and background; subheading sits at 60–70% opacity or a lighter charcoal to create visual rest.
- LOGO: EHN lockup (small, 40–50px, bottom-left corner, opacity ~50–60%) in charcoal or soft green, unobtrusive.

The overall effect should feel calm, clinical, warm, and deeply grounded in EHN's green—never clinical-cold, never the competitor's purple palette.

posts.visual_recipe: A man holds a branded product box (dark red/maroon background with blue "vincent" logo and "Aussie Anti Oxidants" text) while standing in an outdoor urban palm-tree lined plaza on a sunny day. Text overlay at top in bold white impact font reads "WHO IS VINCENT?" The setup is a classic viral reveal/unboxing style creative shot against a scenic background with palm trees, blue sky, and modern building, designed to create intrigue and drive viewer curiosity about the product brand.
posts.angle: Question hook: "What's really hiding in your home?" — reveal-style intro to common mould & microplastic exposure sources in everyday Adelaide living.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Reel/video format — landscape orientation, 30–60 sec hook-to-reveal structure.

Scene composition (mimic winner's outdoor + product reveal energy): A clinician or EHN team member stands in a bright, naturally lit indoor clinic space (or Adelaide courtyard with soft dappled light) holding a visual prop — e.g. a clear glass jar containing soil sample, household item, or water sample demonstrating mould/microplastic concern. Background: warm cream or soft sage wall (not clinical white) with subtle depth, soft natural daylight streaming in. NO palm trees (not Adelaide-native feel); instead, use soft architectural or botanical elements that feel grounded and trusted.

Text overlay (REUSE winner's bold, high-impact approach): Top of frame, large bold Satoshi headline in white or pale cream reads "WHAT'S HIDING IN YOUR HOME?" — place the word "HIDING" or "HOME" in green #39B54A italic bold to anchor the visual.

Colour palette (green-anchored, not red/maroon):
- PRIMARY: EHN green #39B54A as the text highlight + any accents (border, icon, small graphic elements).
- SECONDARY: Warm cream (#F5F2ED or similar), soft sage (#C9D4C8 or muted greens), pale blue-grey (#D4DFE6) — these harmonize with green without competing.
- TEXT: White or pale cream on the overlay; charcoal or dark olive for any lower-third captions.
- NO pure black background; NO bright red, orange, or purple.

Layout density & whitespace (COPY winner's ratio):
- Keep text sparse and bold — one punchy headline, no cluttered captions on the initial hook frame.
- Leave breathing room around the prop and person; don't cram the frame.
- Use 2–3 second hook (visual intrigue + question), then 15–20 sec reveal (clinician points to or discusses the prop/concern).
- Final frame (2–3 sec): soft CTA, e.g. "Concerned about mould or microplastics? Let's investigate" — in smaller Satoshi over a soft cream or sage card with green accent border (thin line, #39B54A).

Typographic hierarchy (match winner's impact):
- Headline: Satoshi Bold 700, large, one word in green italic.
- Body/reveal text: Satoshi Regular 400, smaller, neutral tone.

Feel: Clinical yet approachable, curious rather than alarmist, Australian-natural (soft light, warm materials), never stock-wellness clichés. Mimic the winner's unboxing energy but for functional-medicine insight, not product hype.

posts.visual_recipe: Clean white/off-white background with left-aligned dark grey serif typography. Two text blocks of varying font sizes: header in medium-weight sans-serif introducing the lesser-known history, followed by a larger block of body text in regular weight presenting the Julia Ward Howe narrative. Key phrases underlined for emphasis. Approximately 145 words total. No imagery, faces, or overlays—pure text hierarchy and whitespace.
posts.angle: Honouring the functional-medicine wisdom of the women (and men) who shaped how we nourish ourselves.
posts.build_brief: Canvas: Single vertical square card (1080×1350px, portrait orientation). Background: Warm cream (#F5F3F0) to harmonize softly with EHN green #39B54A and create clinical-but-warm tone. Typography hierarchy: (1) Header in Satoshi Medium (18–20pt), dark charcoal (#2C2C2C), left-aligned, introducing the theme of generational nutrition wisdom. One key word—e.g. "nourished" or "legacy"—in EHN green #39B54A italic bold (700). (2) Body text in Satoshi Regular (14–16pt), charcoal, left-aligned, 4–5 lines presenting a brief personal/clinical reflection on how family eating patterns shape our metabolic health today. Spacing: generous left margin (80–100px), generous right margin (60–80px), approximately 30–40% of card as whitespace. Text block 1 sits ~120px from top; text block 2 begins ~280px from top, leaving breathing room. No imagery, no faces, no overlays—pure text hierarchy. Small EHN logo (40×40px) placed bottom-right corner in soft sage or charcoal. A single subtle accent line in EHN green (#39B54A, 2–3px) placed horizontally above or below the header, sitting 8–10px from text. Total word count: 120–150 words. The overall feel: editorial, clinical, warm—honouring both inherited wisdom and evidence-based practice.
posts.design_recipe: Canvas: Single vertical square card (1080×1350px, portrait orientation). Background: Warm, muted neutral (cream, soft sage, or warm white) chosen to harmonize with and recede behind EHN green #39B54A. Typography hierarchy: (1) Header in Satoshi Medium (18–20pt), dark neutral (charcoal or slate), left-aligned. ONE meaningful word in the headline rendered in EHN green #39B54A italic bold (700) to serve as the visual anchor and draw the eye. (2) Body/supporting text in Satoshi Regular (14–16pt), dark neutral, left-aligned, 4–6 lines. Layout & whitespace: Left margin 80–100px, right margin 60–80px. Text block 1 positioned ~120px from top. Text block 2 positioned ~280px from top, creating a rhythm of content + breathing room. Horizontal accent line in EHN green (#39B54A, 2–3px) placed 8–10px above or below header, acting as a subtle visual anchor without cluttering. No imagery, faces, or overlays—hierarchy and whitespace carry the message. EHN logo (small, 40×40px) bottom-right corner in soft sage or charcoal. Total word count: 120–150 words. Overall feel: editorial, clinical-but-warm, grounded, honouring generational wisdom and evidence-based practice. Colour scheme is green-anchored (one word + one line in #39B54A) with warm, neutral palette supporting readability and calm.

posts.visual_recipe: Text-only Facebook post with no image. Opens with contrarian hook ("Your vet probably isn't telling you this") followed by alarming stat (3 in 4 dogs get cancer; golden retriever lifespan decline). Authority figure introduced (Dr. Judy Morgan, 40-year vet, 9 books). Four bullet-pointed myth-busts on rendered kibble, spaying timing, neurotoxin flea/tick treatments, and vaccine boosters. Closes with CTA to podcast. Structure relies entirely on compelling copy hierarchy and stat-driven urgency to drive engagement and shares. No visual elements—credibility carried by expert credentialing and specific, provocative claims.
posts.angle: Myth-bust household toxins and indoor air quality: expose what your GP might not be telling you about everyday chemical load.

posts.visual_recipe: Clean, minimal white background with black sans-serif typography. Profile avatar (small circular headshot) and verified checkmark in top-left corner. Two lines of bold, large text centred: "Five things I added to my own routine." and "Each takes 5 minutes or less. The research says they each reduce inflammation. None of them is a detox." Simple, authoritative, text-forward design with generous whitespace—no illustrations or overlays.
posts.angle: Five simple morning practices that support stable blood sugar—no detoxes, no extremes, just what the research backs.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Square carousel (5 slides).

Slide 1 (Hook slide):
- Background: Warm cream (#F5F3F0) to harmonize with EHN green and create clinical warmth.
- Top-left corner: Small circular EHN clinic headshot (or subtle EHN avatar, 40px diameter) with minimal grey border, no verification badge needed.
- Centre composition: Two lines of bold Satoshi headline (80–90px weight 700), generous vertical whitespace above and below:
  • Line 1: "Five practices" (with "practices" in EHN green #39B54A, italic 700)
  • Line 2: "I recommend to my own patients." (charcoal #2C2C2C)
- Subline below (Satoshi 18px, weight 400, charcoal): "Each takes 5 minutes. The research backs each one. None is a detox."
- Bottom-right corner: Tiny EHN logo lockup, unobtrusive grey.
- Whitespace-heavy—no illustrations, no overlays. Minimal, authoritative, text-forward.

Slides 2–5 (List slides):
- Background: Soft sage (#E8F0E6) to complement cream + green anchor without clashing.
- Left side: EHN green #39B54A vertical bar or accent (20px wide, full height) as visual anchor.
- Centre: Bold Satoshi headline (60px, weight 700, one key word in green #39B54A italic), e.g. "Protein at breakfast."
- Below headline: Brief supporting line (Satoshi 16px, weight 400, charcoal), e.g. "Stabilises glucose, steadies appetite for hours."
- Small research note (Satoshi 12px, weight 400, soft grey #666): "Why it works: [1–2 plain-language mechanisms]"
- Whitespace mirroring the winner's density—clean, readable, clinical.

Colour story: Cream + sage + EHN green #39B54A as the anchor. Charcoal text. No clashing hues—all harmonize in a natural, editorial palette. Feels grounded, evidence-based, warm.

posts.visual_recipe: A pyramid hierarchy infographic with 6 blue tiers on a white background. Top tier features a coffee cup illustration with beans scattered around, labeled with bold white sans-serif headers and black body text (95 words total). Middle tiers show illustrated spices, dark chocolate, berries (raspberries, strawberries, blueberries, pomegranate, acai), legumes, nuts, and vegetables with labels aligned left and right. Bottom tier displays colorful produce illustrations (leafy greens, beets, broccoli, apples, red cabbage, onions, cherries). Bright green rectangular header box top-right with white text. Brand handle in green text at bottom. Conversational, educational tone via typography hierarchy and colour-coded information density.
posts.angle: The overlooked dietary trigger hiding in plain sight — and how to test it with real certainty.
posts.build_brief:
Create a 6-tier pyramid hierarchy infographic on a warm cream background (#F5F3F0 or similar soft neutral). The pyramid should echo the competitor's vertical composition and whitespace ratio.

**Top tier (widest visual real estate):** Feature a muted sage-green or soft teal illustration of a glass of water or a simplified gut diagram with "fermentation" label. Use bold Satoshi headlines in charcoal; one word ("SIBO") in green #39B54A italic. Body copy in charcoal (95 words max), warm and clinical. Frame the tier with a soft green rectangular header box (using #39B54A or a lighter tint) with white text, positioned top-right.

**Tiers 2–5 (middle section):** Arrange illustrated trigger foods / support foods in left-right alignment, mirroring the competitor's density and layout. Use warm, earthy, grounded line-art illustrations (never glossy stock photos):
- Tier 2: High-FODMAP vegetables (onions, garlic) with left-aligned label
- Tier 3: Fermented foods (sauerkraut, miso) with right-aligned label
- Tier 4: Refined carbs & processed items with left-aligned label
- Tier 5: Low-FODMAP vegetables (carrot, zucchini, lettuce) with right-aligned label

All text in Satoshi, charcoal body; sparse use of green #39B54A as a subtle accent line or icon beside key words.

**Bottom tier (most narrow):** Simple colorful illustrations of gut-friendly whole foods (leafy greens, fish, bone broth, berries, roots) in a single row. Soft, muted colour palette — no neon or clash.

**Overall feel:** Clinical authority + warm accessibility. Whitespace mirrors the competitor's generous margins. Green #39B54A as the unmissable visual anchor (header box, one headline word, subtle accent lines). Background: cream/soft neutral. No pure black text; all charcoal. Logo lockup (small, bottom-left or bottom-right, unobtrusive).

posts.visual_recipe: Cinematic close-up of a hand holding a glossy red apple against a blurred dark background with warm bokeh lighting. A person's face is softly out-of-focus in the background. White bold sans-serif text with black stroke overlay positioned in upper-middle and center of frame reads "Jobs Took a Bite / I Took It Further." The lighting creates a moody, high-contrast aesthetic with the apple as the focal point—approximately 2 sentences of chunked text in heavy font weight (60-70% of frame width).
posts.angle: How the gut microbiome shapes every decision you make — from mood to metabolism.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: vertical reel/video format (9:16), designed for Instagram Reels/TikTok.

Background & lighting: Cinematic close-up of a fermented food item (e.g. sauerkraut in a glass jar, kombucha bottle, or tempeh) with soft, warm bokeh lighting against a charcoal or deep sage-grey background. A blurred human profile (face or silhouette) softly out-of-focus in the background, suggesting the gut–brain connection.

Visual anchor (CRITICAL): The fermented food or jar glows subtly with EHN green #39B54A rim-lighting or a soft green glow emanating from within the jar. This green is the PRIMARY visual anchor—it draws the eye and establishes EHN's brand immediately.

Text placement & hierarchy: White bold sans-serif text (Satoshi 700) with a charcoal or dark sage subtle stroke overlay, positioned in upper-middle and centre of frame. Text reads in 2 chunked lines (60–70% of frame width):
   Line 1 (upper): "You Feed / Your Gut Feeds You"
   (One meaningful word—e.g., "Gut"—rendered in EHN green #39B54A italic.)

Composition: Honour the winner's density and whitespace ratio. Heavy font weight dominates; negative space around the text keeps it cinematic and readable. The fermented food remains the focal point; text overlays it without overwhelming. Warm, moody lighting (never bright or sterile) creates clinical credibility without feeling clinical.

Colour palette (harmonizes with EHN green, never clashes):
   - Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (rim-light, glow, or accent on one headline word)
   - Secondary: Charcoal, deep sage-grey, warm cream, soft taupe
   - NO: pure black, red, purple, bright orange, neon tones
   
Logo placement: Small, discreet EHN lockup in bottom-right corner (never larger than 8% of frame).

Overall feel: Moody, cinematic, grounded—like a functional-medicine textbook meets food photography. Warm bokeh, high contrast, editorial credibility.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1 (hook frame): "You Feed / Your Gut Feeds You" (with "Gut" in EHN green #39B54A italic)

Script outline (2–3 beats):
1. Hook (0–1 sec): Close-up of fermented jar with green glow. Text fades in: "You Feed / Your Gut Feeds You."
2. Intrigue (1–2 sec): Cut to softly-blurred profile of person in background (suggests the gut–brain axis). Voiceover: "Most people think the gut is just digestion."
3. Reveal (2–3 sec): Slow pan on fermented jar, green light intensifies. Text overlay shifts: "Your microbiome is your second brain." (Optional supporting line, smaller Satoshi 500, charcoal.)

posts.visual_recipe: Clean, minimal Twitter-style post layout with Dr. Josh Axe's verified profile photo (circular headshot in light blue shirt) on the left, followed by bold black sans-serif headline stating the 50% natural killer cell stat, contrarian body copy dismissing expensive alternatives, and italicized call-to-action "Two hours outside" at bottom. White background, high contrast typography, no decorative elements.
posts.angle: Sleep quality drives longevity more than we think—and it's not about the hours, it's about nervous-system recovery.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Single square card (1080×1080px) with a cream background (#F5F3F0 or warm off-white).

Layout: Match the winner's Twitter-style minimal structure exactly — clean left-to-right composition with breathing room. 

TOP THIRD (60px top margin): Left-aligned, a circular headshot (120px diameter) of an EHN clinician in warm, natural lighting against a soft sage or cream backdrop. Position this on the LEFT side, leaving right 2/3 of the card open for text hierarchy.

MIDDLE SECTION: Right-aligned text block beginning 140px from left edge, extending to 60px margin on right:
- Headline (Satoshi 700, 52px): "Sleep quality" in black/charcoal (#2A2A2A), then a line break, then "drives longevity" with the word "longevity" in EHN green #39B54A italic 700.
- Stat line (Satoshi 700, 36px, green #39B54A): "People with consistent deep sleep show 40% lower cardiovascular disease risk" — bold, high contrast, sits 20px below headline.

BODY COPY (Satoshi 400, 18px, charcoal #2A2A2A, 30px line-height): Two sentences of contrarian, plain-English copy explaining what deep sleep does to the body (HPA axis, immune recovery, inflammation). Keep it warm, clinical, no hype. Sits 24px below stat, extends to right margin.

BOTTOM THIRD: 40px bottom margin. Italicized call-to-action (Satoshi 400 italic, 20px, green #39B54A): "Start with sleep hygiene." Sit flush left at 140px offset.

VISUAL ANCHORS: 
- Green #39B54A appears in: headline word + stat line + CTA (three visual touchpoints, creating rhythm).
- No decorative elements, no icons, no flourishes.
- White or cream background only.
- Circular headshot is the only image; treated as a clinical asset, not a personality brand.

Overall feel: Clean, minimal, high-contrast, editorial. The green anchors the eye and signals EHN brand without loudness. Whitespace mirrors the winner's restraint. Text density is light, hierarchy is obvious.

posts.visual_recipe: Talking-head style video with male presenter in bright red hoodie against light beige background with dark vertical ribbed wall panel. Bold white outlined text overlay centered on torso reads "I HELPED MY GRANDMA TO DANCE AGAIN" in chunky, playful font. Shelving with supplement bottles and wellness products visible in background right. Microphone visible at frame bottom. High contrast text design with black stroke for legibility.
posts.angle: How functional movement restored walking confidence in a client with chronic knee pain
posts.build_brief: Format: Vertical reel/video (9:16), talking-head style. Clinician presenter (EHN practitioner) in soft sage-green cardigan or natural linen against a warm cream background with a subtle charcoal vertical panelled wall feature (same structural role as the competitor's ribbed wall). Bold outlined text overlay centred on the presenter's torso reads "I HELPED A CLIENT WALK PAIN-FREE AGAIN" in chunky, friendly Satoshi Bold (one word "WALK" in EHN green #39B54A italic to create visual anchor). The word "WALK" acts as the green anchor; the rest is white with fine dark charcoal stroke for legibility on cream. Keep whitespace generous around the text (mirroring the winner's centred composition). Optional: small EHN logo lockup in corner, unobtrusive. Microphone visible at frame bottom echoes the winner's equipment authenticity. Background right: shelf with natural wellness products (herbal jars, books, subtle) — never supplement bottles, keep it clinical and grounded. Tone: warm, hope-filled, clinician-present, high contrast for accessibility.
posts.design_recipe: Format: Vertical reel/video (9:16), talking-head-style structure. Presenter against a warm, muted background (cream, soft sage, or warm grey-blue) with a subtle textured vertical feature (panelled wall) in a complementary neutral (charcoal, soft taupe). Bold, centred text overlay positioned on the presenter's torso in chunky Satoshi Bold. Headline: [placeholder "I HELPED A CLIENT [ACTION] AGAIN"], with ONE meaningful verb/action word in EHN green #39B54A italic to anchor the visual (e.g. "WALK", "MOVE", "THRIVE"). Remaining text in white with fine dark charcoal stroke for contrast and legibility. Generous whitespace around the overlay text (no cluttered density). Optional: small logo lockup, corner placement. Background right: subtle shelf or environmental detail (books, jars, tools) — keep clinical, grounded, never stock-cliché. Equipment visible at frame bottom (microphone, stethoscope, pen) for authenticity. Secondary palette: warm neutrals + the green anchor + optional complementary accent (soft blue-grey, sage, teal) — never clashing or neon. Overall feel: warm, hopeful, clinician-present, high-contrast, accessible.
posts.on_image:
Slide (single reel hook frame):
"I HELPED A CLIENT WALK PAIN-FREE AGAIN" (with "WALK" in EHN green #39B54A italic)

[Video script outline (2-3 beat):
Beat 1 (Hook, 0–2 sec): Presenter in frame, direct eye contact. Text appears on torso: "I HELPED A CLIENT WALK PAIN-FREE AGAIN" with green "WALK".
Beat 2 (Clinician insight, 2–5 sec): Presenter speaks: "Walking is medicine. But when pain gets in the way, we miss out. With the right movement assessment and functional rehab, we can often restore that confidence."
Beat 3 (Soft CTA, 5–8 sec): "If walking feels hard, let's work out why. Book a free discovery call — we're here to listen."]

posts.visual_recipe: Circular headshot of a smiling professional man (Mark Hyman) positioned left, paired with clean sans-serif text on a white background. The opening line is large and bold in black text, followed by a listicle-style breakdown of five parallel statements (laughter/crying/hugging/singing/dancing) with scientific terms, ending with a thesis statement. Minimalist, high-contrast layout with generous white space; professional authority presentation without visual clutter.
posts.angle: Vagal tone and nervous system regulation: the biological bridge between gut health and mental wellbeing
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Single square image (1080×1080px). Background: Warm cream (#F5F1ED or similar soft neutral that complements EHN green #39B54A). Layout: Circular headshot of a warm, calm clinician (or relatable patient/practitioner in grounded lighting) positioned left-centre, approximately 280px diameter, with soft edge. Text on right: Headline in bold Satoshi 700, 48–56px, charcoal (#2C2C2C), reading "Your gut is listening" with the word "listening" in green #39B54A italic 700. Below, 12–16px body line in charcoal: "Here's what the science says." Then 5 short lines (18–20px, Satoshi 500, charcoal), single-spaced with 18px line gaps, listing parallel statements:
• Stress signals → vagal tone drops
• Poor digestion → nervous system stays triggered
• Tight shoulders → shallow breathing loop
• Dysbiosis → inflammatory cytokines rise
• Healing gut → vagus fires again

Thesis statement below (16px, Satoshi 600, charcoal with green accent): "Your nervous system and microbiome are in constant dialogue. Feed one, you feed the other." Whitespace ratio: ~20% cream breathing room on right and below. Minimal visual clutter. EHN logo (small, bottom-right corner, 60px width). No borders; soft, clinical authority without sterility. Total composition: left-anchored headshot, clean right-aligned text block, generous whitespace, green as the sole accent colour.
posts.design_recipe: Canvas: Single square image (1080×1080px). Background: Warm, neutral cream or soft sage—chosen to harmonize with EHN green #39B54A and suit the post topic (never pure white, never pure black, never competitor's palette). Layout: Circular headshot positioned left-centre (280–320px diameter, soft edge), paired with right-aligned text block. Headline: bold Satoshi 700, 48–56px, charcoal or warm neutral, with ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic 700. Below headline: 12–16px body line (plain Satoshi 500, charcoal), introducing the listicle. Then: 5 parallel short lines (18–20px, Satoshi 500, charcoal), single-spaced with 18px gaps between lines, each line presenting a statement or concept without numbering. Below listicle: Thesis statement (16px, Satoshi 600, charcoal) with green #39B54A as the only visual accent (highlight one key word or phrase). Whitespace: ~20% cream/neutral breathing room (right side and below text block). EHN logo lockup (small, unobtrusive, bottom-right, 60px width). No borders, no clutter. Tone: Clinical, warm, editorial, grounded. Green #39B54A as the sole visual anchor; secondary palette (cream, charcoal, warm neutrals) must harmonize with green and never clash. Overall feel: professional authority, myth-busting register, high contrast, generous whitespace, validation-focused.
